body.title: BaseShim: two-phase dispatch handshake protocol
body.result: BaseShim: _DispatchHandshake + receive_dispatch; ack synchronous, prod every 120s, timeout at 600s; 15 tests green
body.intention: I intend that BaseShim handles the receive→ack→prod→timeout cycle so every worker gets the handshake for free and no app code needs to know about it.
body.description:
Workers currently have no way to signal Granny that a dispatch envelope was received but the app is busy. Add a two-phase protocol to BaseShim: (1) on receiving a dispatch envelope, shim immediately sends 'got it' back and flips ticket to `acked`; (2) shim prods the app every 2 minutes; when app picks up, shim sends 'started' and flips to `in_progress`; (3) if no pickup after 10 minutes from ack, shim sends 'timeout' and Granny marks `escalated`. The app sees none of this — it just gets called when it's ready.

**Affected files:** `unseen_university/shim.py` (BaseShim — add dispatch receive handler, ack send, prod loop, timeout), `tests/test_shim_output_validation.py` or new `tests/test_shim_handshake.py`
**Design rules:** none apply (shim-internal protocol; no DB schema changes here)
**Scope boundary:** Protocol lives in BaseShim only. Granny's side of the handshake (sending envelopes, reading responses) is T-granny-bus-dispatch. Queue status additions are T-queue-dispatched-status (gate: must ship first).
**Completion criteria:** Unit tests show: (a) dispatch envelope → ack sent within 50ms; (b) prod fires every 120s when app hasn't picked up; (c) timeout fires at 600s and sends timeout envelope; (d) app picks up → started envelope sent and prod loop stops.
